base_attributes.description: Unique identifier for the pipe segment. Following plant standard tagging convention.
Piping Line Number to which the pipe segment belongs. Following plant standard line numbering convention.
Nominal Pipe Size (NPS) or Diameter Nominal (DN) of the pipe. Specify unit clearly (e.g., "NPS 6 inch" or "DN 150 mm").
Pipe schedule indicating wall thickness (e.g., SCH 40, SCH 80, STD, XS, XXS). Based on ASME B36.10M or ASME B36.19M.
Reference to the material specification document or material code. Specifies the material grade and standard (e.g., ASTM A106 Grade B).
base_attributes.notes: Required: YES. Data type is a reference.

base_attributes.description: Length of the pipe segment.
Actual wall thickness of the pipe.
Maximum allowable pressure for which the pipe is designed.
Maximum and Minimum allowable temperature for which the pipe is designed. Specify both if applicable, or range.
Normal operating pressure in the pipe segment.
Normal operating temperature in the pipe segment.
Description of the fluid service (e.g., "Process Water," "Crude Oil," "Steam").
Type of insulation applied to the pipe, if any (e.g., "Mineral Wool," "Calcium Silicate," "None").
Thickness of the insulation layer, if applicable.
Specification for painting or coating applied to the pipe for corrosion protection or identification.
P&ID symbol representation for the pipe segment (e.g., straight line, dashed line).
Drawing number where the pipe segment is detailed.
Date of installation of the pipe segment.
Date of the last inspection performed on the pipe segment.
Unique identifier for the pipe spool. Used for fabrication and installation tracking.
Name of the fabrication shop that manufactured the spool.
Drawing number specifically for the spool fabrication.
Weight of the pipe spool.
Unique identifier for the fitting. Following plant standard tagging convention.
Piping Line Number to which the fitting belongs.
Type of fitting (e.g., Elbow, Tee, Reducer, Coupling, Cap, Plug, Union, Nipple). Derived from the taxonomy.
Nominal Pipe Size (NPS) or Diameter Nominal (DN) of the fitting. Specify unit clearly (e.g., "NPS 6 inch" or "DN 150 mm").
Reference to the material specification document or material code. Specifies the material grade and standard (e.g., ASTM A234 Grade WPB).
base_attributes.notes: Required: YES. Data type is a reference.

base_attributes.description: Fitting schedule or pressure rating (e.g., SCH 40, SCH 80, Class 150, Class 300). Based on applicable standards.
Type of connection (e.g., "Welded," "Threaded," "Flanged," "Socket Weld").
Maximum allowable pressure for which the fitting is designed.
Maximum and Minimum allowable temperature for which the fitting is designed. Specify both if applicable, or range.
Normal operating pressure in the fitting location.
Normal operating temperature in the fitting location.
Description of the fluid service (inherited from the line).
Specification for painting or coating, if applicable.
P&ID symbol representation for the fitting.
Drawing number where the fitting is detailed.
Date of installation of the fitting.
Date of the last inspection performed on the fitting.
Specific type of elbow (e.g., 90_degree_elbow, 45_degree_elbow, long_radius_elbow, short_radius_elbow). Derived from taxonomy.
Angle of the bend in degrees (e.g., 90, 45). Applicable to elbows with specific angles.
Radius type (e.g., "Long Radius," "Short Radius"). Applicable to elbows with radius designations.
Radius of the elbow bend. If radius_type is specified, provide the radius value.
Specific type of tee (e.g., straight_tee, reducing_tee). Derived from taxonomy.
Diameter of the branch connection of a reducing tee. Applicable only to reducing tees.
Specific type of reducer (e.g., concentric_reducer, eccentric_reducer). Derived from taxonomy.
Nominal diameter of the smaller end of the reducer.
Nominal diameter of the larger end of the reducer.
Offset between the centerlines of the two ends for eccentric reducers. Applicable only to eccentric reducers.
Specific type of coupling (e.g., full_coupling, half_coupling, reducing_coupling). Derived from taxonomy.
Nominal diameter of the smaller end for reducing couplings. Applicable only to reducing couplings.
Nominal diameter of the larger end for reducing couplings. Applicable only to reducing couplings.
Length of the nipple.
Unique identifier for the flange. Following plant standard tagging convention.
Piping Line Number to which the flange is associated (if applicable - flanges can be stand-alone components).
Type of flange (e.g., welding_neck_flange, slip_on_flange, blind_flange, threaded_flange, socket_weld_flange, lap_joint_flange). Derived from taxonomy.
Nominal Pipe Size (NPS) or Diameter Nominal (DN) of the flange. Specify unit clearly (e.g., "NPS 6 inch" or "DN 150 mm").
Reference to the material specification document or material code. Specifies the material grade and standard (e.g., ASTM A105).
base_attributes.notes: Required: YES. Data type is a reference.

base_attributes.description: Pressure class or rating of the flange (e.g., Class 150, Class 300, PN16, PN40). Based on ASME B16.5 or EN 1092-1.
